name: BitMart Crypto Exchange API
what_it_does: Cayman Islands-based cryptocurrency exchange with REST and WebSocket API — enabling traders and developers to access spot trading, futures, leveraged ETFs, market data, and account management, with HMAC-SHA256 authentication and official SDKs in multiple languages.
use_cases: Executing spot trades via BitMart API for cryptocurrency algorithmic trading strategies
Managing futures positions via BitMart API for leveraged derivatives trading
Accessing market data via BitMart API for real-time price feeds and order book analysis
Streaming real-time data via BitMart WebSocket for live trading applications
Integrating BitMart via official SDK for multi-language trading bot development
not_for: US-based users (BitMart has restrictions for US users; limited NYS BitLicense compliance)
Top-tier liquidity for major pairs (use Binance/Coinbase)
best_when: A developer needs official SDK support in multiple languages and a broader altcoin selection, with decent documentation and code examples for bot development.
avoid_when: You need US compliance, institutional-grade security assurances, or top-tier liquidity. Note: BitMart suffered a $196M hack in 2021.

auth.notes: HMAC-SHA256 signature. Headers: X-BM-KEY, X-BM-SIGN, X-BM-TIMESTAMP. Signature: HMAC-SHA256 of timestamp + '#' + memo + '#' + request body. Memo: API key memo string (set when creating API key). Timestamp: Unix milliseconds. REST API at api-cloud.bitmart.com/. WebSocket: wss://ws-manager-compress.bitmart.com/. Official SDKs: Python, Java, PHP, Node.js, Go. Spot API v2 and v3 available. Futures API: separate endpoint.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; spot trading: 0.25% standard maker/taker
pricing.paid_tiers: BMX token for fee discounts; VIP tiers by volume
pricing.notes: Cayman Islands company. Founded 2018. CRITICAL: December 2021 — $196M hack (hot wallet compromise). Full reimbursement claimed. BMX token: native exchange token for fee discounts. Products: Spot, Futures, Leveraged ETFs (3x tokens), Launchpad. Official SDKs in 6 languages (Python, Java, PHP, Node.js, Go) on GitHub (github.com/bitmartexchange) — rare for mid-tier exchange. ~900+ trading pairs. API documentation: developer-pro.bitmart.com. Memo field: user-defined string added to API key for signature (security feature). Post-2021 hack recovery: implemented cold storage improvements.

performance.rate_limits: Spot: 100 req/12 sec per IP; varies by endpoint

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: X-BM-KEY/SIGN/TIMESTAMP headers. Memo field in signature (unusual). HMAC of timestamp+'#'+memo+'#'+body. $196M hack 2021. Official SDKs in 6 languages. Cayman Islands company. Futures separate endpoint. v2/v3 spot API coexist.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET requests idempotent. Order placement: use client_order_id for idempotent tracking. Market data reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Memo field: user-defined string set at API key creation — MUST be included in signature (timestamp+'#'+memo+'#'+body)
Spot API has v2 and v3 endpoints coexisting — check documentation for which to use for each operation
$196M hack (Dec 2021): evaluate post-hack security improvements before production integration
Futures API is a SEPARATE endpoint with different base URL from spot API
Official SDKs available on GitHub (bitmartexchange) — use these for correct HMAC implementation
US restrictions: verify access from your region — US users have limited access

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HMAC-SHA256 with memo field — adds user-specific token to signature. Cayman Islands: offshore jurisdiction, limited regulatory oversight. $196M hack (2021): hot wallet compromise — verify cold storage improvements. Official SDKs in 6 languages — correct HMAC implementation. API key memo: partial additional factor. Timestamp prevents replay attacks. Post-hack security improvements claimed.
